$comment: Mechanically-enforced gate baselines (see EVALS.md rules). run-routing.mjs prints a gate verdict on every run and exits 1 on breach when invoked with --gate (CI does). Re-baselining is an explicit act: update these numbers in the same commit as the change that moves them, with the decision recorded in Solo (EVALS.md rule 1). Counts are absolute hits, not percentages.
note: re-baselined 2026-07-03 (afternoon) on M3/M4 operation keywords (Solo todo 824 items 4+5): operations gained the low-weight `keywords` field, distilled from schema property names/enum values plus (stellarDocs) the page-title snapshot in inventory/stellar-docs-titles.json, document-frequency-filtered so only op-distinguishing vocabulary survives. Legacy 219/287/313→222/288/318, extended strict top1 74→77 top5 106→109, skills lane unchanged 18/23 — ZERO per-case hit→miss regressions in any lane vs routing-2026-07-03T14-37-46-628Z (13 case-improvements). A naive variant without the DF filter measured NEGATIVE (extended top1 74→71, skills top5 23→22) and was rejected. Prior baseline: routing-2026-07-03T13-59-06-937Z.json (onboarding-skills retirement, todo 825: skills lane 31→23 cases, legacy unchanged 219/287/313).
